_methodology: Direct test of whether keyword scoring degrades more than judge scoring under perturbation. Pre-registered for inclusion in Phase 1.5 combined pass-flip findings.
recommended_methodology_page_text: Across the 3,195 eligible runs that share the assumption-compliance rubric, keyword scoring degrades faster than the LLM-judge under perturbation: keyword PASS rate drops 1.9pp (from 68.7% on base to 66.8% on perturbation), while judge PASS rate drops only -1.8pp (from 48.6% to 50.4%). The 3.7pp differential indicates that surface-form changes — rephrasing, numerical reseeding, semantic reframing — disproportionately break the keyword regex, whereas the judge rubric tracks the underlying mathematical content more robustly. This is direct empirical support for using LLM-as-a-judge scoring as the canonical assumption-compliance metric on perturbation-evaluated tasks.
